prerequisites: A running Amazon Redshift provisioned cluster or Redshift Serverless workgroup that holds the data you want to read.
The cluster endpoint (host) and the TCP port it listens on. Redshift uses port 5439 by default.
The name of the database on the cluster to connect to (for example, the default dev database).
A Redshift database user name and that user's password with read access to the schemas and tables you intend to extract.
Network reachability from where pm runs to the cluster: an inbound security group ingress rule allowing your client's IP/CIDR on the cluster port, plus any firewall ports opened on your side.

authMethods.name: Database user name and password
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Redshift database user name (username) and that user's password. The password is the connector's only secret field; it is supplied to the Redshift JDBC connection along with the host, port, and database. AWS documents this as authenticating the connection using a database user name and password (for example UID and PWD in the JDBC URL).

setupSteps.title: Locate the cluster endpoint and port
setupSteps.body: In the Amazon Redshift console, open your cluster and copy its endpoint. Use the endpoint as the host value and the cluster's port as the port value. Redshift listens on port 5439 by default, and the port cannot be changed after the cluster is created. Always connect using the Redshift endpoint rather than a node or VPC endpoint IP address.

setupSteps.title: Identify the database
setupSteps.body: Set database to the name of the database you want to read from on the cluster (for example, the default dev database). A Redshift database contains one or more named schemas, each holding tables and other objects.

setupSteps.title: Create or choose a database user and grant read access
setupSteps.body: Use an existing Redshift database user or create one, then grant it SELECT access to the schemas and tables you want to extract. Enter that user as username and its password as the password secret field.

setupSteps.title: Allow network access to the cluster
setupSteps.body: Ensure the machine running pm can reach the cluster on its port. Add an inbound ingress rule to the security group attached to the cluster that allows your client's IP/CIDR on the cluster port, and confirm the same port is open in any firewall on your side.

setupSteps.title: Optionally restrict schemas and tune the JDBC connection
setupSteps.body: Set the optional schemas field to limit extraction to specific Redshift schemas. Use the optional jdbc_url_params field to append extra Redshift JDBC connection options (for example ssl=true), which are added to the connection URL as semicolon- or ampersand-separated property=value pairs.

sources.title: Building the connection URL - Amazon Redshift (Management Guide)
